seoTitle: How to Set Up a Wix Payments Account
metaDescription: Learn how to activate Wix Payments, connect your bank account, and complete verification so you can accept card payments.
intro: Wix Payments is the platform's native gateway for accepting credit and debit card transactions directly on your site. This guide covers activating it from your dashboard, entering your business and banking details, and completing the verification steps required to start processing payments without holds or delays.

steps.heading: Open your Wix dashboard
steps.body: Navigate to your site's settings and locate the finance preferences area.

steps.heading: Go to Accept Payments
steps.body: Open the Accept Payments configuration panel to see available gateway providers.

steps.heading: Select Wix Payments
steps.body: Choose Wix Payments as your primary gateway and confirm it's available in your region.

steps.heading: Fill out your business details
steps.body: Enter your business registration information, address, and tax details in the merchant identity form.

steps.heading: Connect your bank account
steps.body: Input your bank account and routing numbers to set up payout distribution.

steps.heading: Complete verification
steps.body: Submit any required verification documents and review your checkout display settings to avoid transaction holds.

faq.question: Is Wix Payments available in every country?
faq.answer: No, availability depends on your business location, so it's worth checking during setup whether the gateway supports your region.

faq.question: How long does verification take?
faq.answer: Verification timing varies, but completing the compliance check accurately upfront helps avoid transaction holds or payout delays.

faq.question: Do I need a registered business to use Wix Payments?
faq.answer: You'll be asked for business registration and tax information as part of the merchant onboarding form, so having that ready speeds things up.

faq.question: Can I change my payout bank account later?
faq.answer: Yes, you can typically update your connected bank account details from the same payments settings panel after initial setup.
